docs.title: picsure
docs.text: Python client for the PIC-SURE API. Search the data dictionary, build cohort queries with nested AND/OR filters, and export participant-level data — all from a Jupyter notebook.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Connect to any PIC-SURE instance with a single function call
  • Search the data dictionary and narrow results with facet filters
  • Build queries with nested AND/OR clause groups for complex cohort selection
  • Run queries to get counts, participant-level data, or time series
  • Export results as CSV, TSV, or PFB

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
import picsure
from picsure import ClauseType, GroupOperator

# Connect
session = picsure.connect(platform="BDC Authorized", token=my_token)

# Search the dictionary
results = session.search("blood pressure")

# Build a query
sex = picsure.createClause(
    "\\phs1\\sex\\", type=ClauseType.FILTER, categories="Male"
)
age = picsure.createClause(
    "\\phs1\\age\\", type=ClauseType.FILTER, min=40
)
query = picsure.buildClauseGroup([sex, age], root=GroupOperator.AND)

# Run and export
count_result = session.runQuery(query, type="count")
count = count_result.value  # None if suppressed; check count_result.cap
df = session.runQuery(query, type="participant")
session.exportCSV(df, "cohort.csv")

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install picsure

For PFB export support:

pip install picsure[pfb]

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Getting Started — connect and run your first query
  • User Guides — detailed walkthroughs
  • API Reference — complete function documentation
  • Migration Guide — upgrading from the old adapter

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks you through connecting to PIC-SURE, searching the data dictionary, building a query, and exporting results.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10 or later
  • A PIC-SURE API token (generate one here)
  • pip install picsure

docs.title: Step 1: Connect
docs.text:
import picsure

session = picsure.connect(
    platform="BDC Authorized",
    token="your-api-token",
)

You'll see a confirmation message:

You're successfully connected to BDC Authorized as user you@email.com!
Your token expires on 2026-06-15T00:00:00Z.

The platform parameter accepts named platforms ("BDC Authorized", "BDC Open", "Demo") or a custom URL for other PIC-SURE instances.

docs.title: Explicit Cleanup (Recommended)
docs.text:
Session is a context manager. In scripts or long-running processes, use with so the underlying HTTP connection pool is released promptly:

with picsure.connect(platform="BDC Authorized", token="your-api-token") as session:
    df = session.search("blood pressure")
    # connection released on exit

This is not required in short-lived notebook cells where the kernel shuts down soon after; it is recommended when the script runs repeatedly or lives inside a long-running process. You can also call session.close() directly.

docs.title: Step 2: Search the Data Dictionary
docs.text:
# Search for variables containing "blood pressure"
results = session.search("blood pressure")
results.head()

The result is a pandas DataFrame with columns like conceptPath, name, display, description, dataType, studyId, and values.

docs.title: Narrowing with Facets
docs.text:
# See all available facet categories
session.showAllFacets()

# Create a facet filter
facets = session.facets()
facets.add("dataset_id", "phs000007")

# Search with facets
fhs_results = session.search("blood pressure", facets=facets)

docs.title: Step 3: Build a Query
docs.text:
from picsure import ClauseType, GroupOperator

# Create individual clauses
sex_filter = picsure.createClause(
    "\\phs1\\pht1\\phv1\\sex\\",
    type=ClauseType.FILTER,
    categories="Male",
)

age_filter = picsure.createClause(
    "\\phs1\\pht1\\phv5\\age\\",
    type=ClauseType.FILTER,
    min=40,
)

# Combine into a group
query = picsure.buildClauseGroup(
    [sex_filter, age_filter],
    root=GroupOperator.AND,
)

Queries can be nested arbitrarily deep. See the Building Queries guide for more examples.

docs.title: Step 4: Run the Query
docs.text:
# Get a count of matching participants
count_result = session.runQuery(query, type="count")
if count_result.value is not None:
    print(f"{count_result.value} participants match")
else:
    print(f"fewer than {count_result.cap} participants match (suppressed)")

# Get participant-level data
df = session.runQuery(query, type="participant")
df.head()

docs.title: Step 5: Export Results
docs.text:
# Save as CSV
session.exportCSV(df, "my_cohort.csv")

# Save as TSV
session.exportTSV(df, "my_cohort.tsv")

# Export as PFB (requires pip install picsure[pfb])
session.exportPFB(query, "my_cohort.pfb")

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Search & Facets Guide — advanced search patterns
  • Building Queries Guide — nested AND/OR queries
  • API Reference — complete function documentation

docs.title: Building Queries
docs.text: This guide covers creating filter clauses and combining them into nested AND/OR query groups.

docs.title: Clause Types
docs.text:
PIC-SURE supports four types of clauses:

Type Purpose Example FILTER Filter by value or range Males only, age > 40 ANYRECORD Match any record with a value Has any BMI measurement SELECT Include in output, no filtering Add height to results REQUIRE Require non-null value Must have blood pressure

docs.title: Creating Clauses
docs.text:
from picsure import createClause, ClauseType

# Categorical filter
sex = createClause(
    "\\phs1\\pht1\\phv1\\sex\\",
    type=ClauseType.FILTER,
    categories="Male",
)

# Multiple categorical values
asthma = createClause(
    "\\phs1\\pht2\\phv3\\asthma\\",
    type=ClauseType.FILTER,
    categories=["Yes, recent", "Yes, since childhood"],
)

# Numeric range filter
age = createClause(
    "\\phs1\\pht1\\phv5\\age\\",
    type=ClauseType.FILTER,
    min=40,
    max=80,
)

# Min only (no upper bound)
age_over_40 = createClause(
    "\\phs1\\pht1\\phv5\\age\\",
    type=ClauseType.FILTER,
    min=40,
)

# Any record of a variable
has_sleep_data = createClause(
    "\\phs1\\pht3\\phv8\\trouble_sleeping\\",
    type=ClauseType.ANYRECORD,
)

# Select for output without filtering
include_height = createClause(
    "\\phs1\\pht1\\phv10\\height\\",
    type=ClauseType.SELECT,
)

docs.title: Combining Clauses with AND/OR
docs.text:
Use buildClauseGroup to combine clauses:

from picsure import buildClauseGroup, GroupOperator

# AND: all conditions must be true
males_over_40 = buildClauseGroup(
    [sex, age_over_40],
    root=GroupOperator.AND,
)

# OR: at least one condition must be true
copd_or_asthma = buildClauseGroup(
    [copd, asthma],
    root=GroupOperator.OR,
)

docs.title: Nesting Groups
docs.text:
Groups can contain other groups for complex logic:

# Find males over 40 with COPD or asthma
full_query = buildClauseGroup(
    [males_over_40, copd_or_asthma],
    root=GroupOperator.AND,
)

This produces the logical expression:

(sex = Male AND age >= 40) AND (COPD = Yes OR asthma in [Yes, recent; Yes, since childhood])

docs.title: Full Example from the Product Spec
docs.text:
Select participants who are male, over 40, and have either COPD/asthma or sleep problems:

import picsure
from picsure import createClause, buildClauseGroup, ClauseType, GroupOperator

sex_filter = createClause("\\phs1\\sex\\", type=ClauseType.FILTER, categories="Male")
age_filter = createClause("\\phs1\\age\\", type=ClauseType.FILTER, min=40)
copd_filter = createClause("\\phs1\\copd\\", type=ClauseType.FILTER, categories="Yes")
asthma_filter = createClause("\\phs1\\asthma\\", type=ClauseType.FILTER, categories="Yes")
sleep_filter = createClause("\\phs1\\trouble_sleeping\\", type=ClauseType.ANYRECORD)
insomnia_filter = createClause("\\phs1\\insomnia\\", type=ClauseType.ANYRECORD)

copd_or_asthma = buildClauseGroup([copd_filter, asthma_filter], root=GroupOperator.OR)
sleep_or_insomnia = buildClauseGroup([sleep_filter, insomnia_filter], root=GroupOperator.OR)

full_query = buildClauseGroup(
    [sex_filter, age_filter, copd_or_asthma, sleep_or_insomnia],
    root=GroupOperator.AND,
)

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
The library validates clause configurations and provides actionable error messages:

# ANYRECORD with categories raises an error
createClause("\\path\\", type=ClauseType.ANYRECORD, categories="Male")
# PicSureValidationError: ANYRECORD clauses cannot have categories.
# ANYRECORD matches the presence of any value for the variable.
# Remove the categories argument.

# FILTER without any criteria raises an error
createClause("\\path\\", type=ClauseType.FILTER)
# PicSureValidationError: FILTER clauses require at least one of:
# categories, min, or max.

docs.title: Inspecting Query JSON
docs.text:
Every clause and group can serialize to the v3 query JSON format:

import json
print(json.dumps(full_query.to_query_json(), indent=2))

docs.title: Migrating from PicSureHpdsLib
docs.text: This guide maps the old PicSureHpdsLib API to the new picsure package. The new package is a complete rewrite — old import paths will not work.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Old:

pip install git+https://github.com/hms-dbmi/pic-sure-python-client.git
pip install git+https://github.com/hms-dbmi/pic-sure-python-adapter-hpds.git

New:

pip install picsure

One package replaces both the client and the adapter.

docs.title: Quick Reference
docs.text: Old API New API PicSureClient.Client(url, token) picsure.connect(platform, token) PicSureHpdsLib.Adapter(conn) (not needed — connect returns a Session directly) adapter.useResource(uuid) session.setResourceID(uuid) resource.dictionary().find("sex") session.search("sex") resource.query() picsure.createClause(...) query.filter().add(path, values) picsure.createClause(path, type=ClauseType.FILTER, categories=values) query.require().add(path) picsure.createClause(path, type=ClauseType.REQUIRE) query.anyRecordOf().add(path) picsure.createClause(path, type=ClauseType.ANYRECORD) query.select().add(path) picsure.createClause(path, type=ClauseType.SELECT) query.getCount() session.runQuery(query, type="count") query.getResults() session.runQuery(query, type="participant") query.getResultsDataFrame() session.runQuery(query, type="participant")

docs.title: Return-type changes
docs.text: Note: runQuery(..., type="count") now returns a CountResult, not an int. Use result.value for the numeric count (which may be None on open-access deployments that suppress small counts — check result.cap in that case).

docs.title: Connecting
docs.text:
Old:

import PicSureClient
import PicSureHpdsLib

conn = PicSureClient.Client(url="https://picsure.biodatacatalyst.nhlbi.nih.gov/picsure/", token=my_token)
adapter = PicSureHpdsLib.Adapter(conn)
resource = adapter.useResource(resource_uuid)

New:

import picsure

session = picsure.connect(platform="BDC Authorized", token=my_token)

docs.title: Searching the Dictionary
docs.text:
Old:

dictionary = resource.dictionary()
results = dictionary.find("blood pressure")
results.DataFrame()

New:

df = session.search("blood pressure")

docs.title: Building a Query
docs.text:
Old:

query = resource.query()
query.filter().add("\\phs1\\sex\\", ["Male"])
query.filter().add("\\phs1\\age\\", min=40)
query.require().add("\\phs1\\bmi\\")

New:

from picsure import createClause, buildClauseGroup, ClauseType, GroupOperator

sex = createClause("\\phs1\\sex\\", type=ClauseType.FILTER, categories="Male")
age = createClause("\\phs1\\age\\", type=ClauseType.FILTER, min=40)
bmi = createClause("\\phs1\\bmi\\", type=ClauseType.REQUIRE)

query = buildClauseGroup([sex, age, bmi], root=GroupOperator.AND)

docs.title: Running a Query
docs.text:
Old:

count = query.getCount()
df = query.getResultsDataFrame()

New:

count_result = session.runQuery(query, type="count")
if count_result.value is not None:
    print(f"{count_result.value} participants match")
else:
    print(f"fewer than {count_result.cap} participants match (suppressed)")
df = session.runQuery(query, type="participant")

docs.title: Exporting
docs.text:
Old:

df.to_csv("output.csv")

New:

session.exportCSV(df, "output.csv")
# or
session.exportTSV(df, "output.tsv")
# or
session.exportPFB(query, "output.pfb")

docs.title: Removed
docs.text:
• Consents handling — now managed by the backend as part of advanced filtering. No adapter-side consent logic needed.
  • BypassAdapter — direct HPDS connections are no longer supported. All connections go through PIC-SURE.
  • Two-package install — the client and adapter are merged into one package.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Facet filtering — narrow search results by study, data type, etc.
  • Nested AND/OR queries — build complex cohort logic with clause groups.
  • Named platforms — connect with "BDC Authorized" instead of a URL.
  • PFB export — export query results in PFB format.
  • Time series queries — retrieve longitudinal data.
  • Actionable error messages — every error tells you what went wrong and how to fix it.

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text: If you run into issues migrating, check the API Reference for complete function documentation or open an issue on GitHub.

docs.title: Running Queries & Exporting Data
docs.text: This guide covers executing queries and saving results.

docs.title: Running Queries
docs.text: Use session.runQuery() with your query and a result type:

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
Get the number of matching participants:

count_result = session.runQuery(my_query, type="count")
if count_result.value is not None:
    print(f"{count_result.value} participants match")
else:
    print(f"fewer than {count_result.cap} participants match (suppressed)")

Returns a CountResult. Access the numeric count via .value (which may be None on open-access deployments that suppress small counts — check .cap in that case).

docs.title: Participant Data
docs.text:
Get participant-level data as a DataFrame:

df = session.runQuery(my_query, type="participant")
df.head()

Returns a pandas DataFrame with one row per participant.

docs.title: Time Series Data
docs.text:
Get time-series data as a DataFrame:

df = session.runQuery(my_query, type="timestamp")
df.head()

Returns a pandas DataFrame with repeated measurements over time.

docs.title: CSV
docs.text:
df = session.runQuery(my_query, type="participant")
session.exportCSV(df, "my_cohort.csv")

docs.title: TSV
docs.text: session.exportTSV(df, "my_cohort.tsv")

docs.title: PFB
docs.text:
PFB export runs the query server-side and writes the result directly to a file:

session.exportPFB(my_query, "my_cohort.pfb")

Note

PFB export requires the optional pypfb dependency: 

pip install picsure[pfb]

docs.title: Simple Queries
docs.text:
You can pass a single clause directly to runQuery — you don't have to wrap it in a group first:

sex = picsure.createClause("\\phs1\\sex\\", type=ClauseType.FILTER, categories="Male")

# This works — no buildClauseGroup needed for simple queries
count_result = session.runQuery(sex, type="count")
count = count_result.value  # None if suppressed; check count_result.cap

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
If something goes wrong, you'll get a clear error message:

# Invalid query type
session.runQuery(my_query, type="invalid")
# PicSureValidationError: 'invalid' is not a valid query type.
# Valid types: count, participant, timestamp.

Researchers don't need to write try/except blocks. The error messages tell you what happened and what to do about it.

docs.title: Search & Facets
docs.text: This guide covers searching the PIC-SURE data dictionary and using facets to narrow results.

docs.title: Basic Search
docs.text:
Search for variables by keyword. Results are returned as a pandas DataFrame.

results = session.search("blood pressure")
print(f"Found {len(results)} variables")
results.head()

docs.title: Search with No Term
docs.text:
Pass an empty string (or no argument) to return all variables:

all_vars = session.search()

docs.title: Exclude Values
docs.text:
For faster searches on large dictionaries, set include_values=False to omit the values column:

results = session.search("age", include_values=False)

docs.title: Facet Filtering
docs.text: Facets let you narrow search results by category — for example, filtering to variables from a specific study.

docs.title: View Available Facets
docs.text:
# See all facet categories and their options
session.showAllFacets()

This returns a DataFrame with six columns:

Column Meaning category Category identifier (e.g. dataset_id) — pass this to FacetSet.add. Category Display Human-readable category label (e.g. Dataset). display Facet option's display label. description Facet option's description, if any. value Option identifier — pass this to FacetSet.add. count Number of concepts matching this option.

docs.title: Build a Facet Filter
docs.text:
# Create a FacetSet with available categories
facets = session.facets()

# See current (empty) selections
facets.view()
# {'dataset_id': [], 'data_type': [], ...}

# Add a study filter
facets.add("dataset_id", "phs000007")

# Add multiple values at once
facets.add("data_type", ["categorical", "continuous"])

# Use in search
filtered = session.search("blood pressure", facets=facets)

docs.title: Clear Facet Selections
docs.text:
# Clear one category
facets.clear("dataset_id")

# Clear all selections
facets.clear()

docs.title: Invalid Facets
docs.text:
If you pass an invalid category name, you'll get a clear error message listing the valid options:

facets.add("invalid_category", "value")
# PicSureValidationError: 'invalid_category' is not a valid facet
# category. Valid categories: data_type, dataset_id.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for all public functions, classes, and types in the picsure package.

docs.title: connect
docs.text:
connect(
    platform: Platform | str,
    token: str = "",
    resource_uuid: str | None = None,
    *,
    include_consents: bool | None = None,
    requires_auth: bool | None = None,
    dev_mode: bool | None = None,
) -> Session

Connect to a PIC-SURE instance and return a Session.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default platform Platform | str

A :class:Platform enum member (e.g. Platform.BDC_AUTHORIZED) or a full URL (e.g. "https://my-picsure.example.com").

required token str

Your PIC-SURE API token. Leave empty for open-access platforms (e.g. Platform.BDC_OPEN) that don't require authentication.

'' resource_uuid str | None

Optional resource UUID to use. Overrides the default UUID from the Platform enum. Required for custom URLs — if omitted, call session.setResourceID(uuid) after reviewing session.getResourceID().

None include_consents bool | None

Override the platform's consent policy. For known Platform members this defaults to the member's own flag; for custom URLs it defaults to False. Pass True to fetch the consent list from PSAMA on connect.

None requires_auth bool | None

Override the platform's auth requirement. Known Platform members default to their own flag; custom URLs default to True. Pass False to skip the PSAMA profile call on an open-access deployment.

None dev_mode bool | None

Developer-mode toggle. None (default) defers to the PICSURE_DEV_MODE env var; True / False overrides it. When on, events for every HTTP call and public Session method are captured in an in-memory buffer, and a default stderr handler is attached to the picsure logger (unless one already exists).

None

Returns:

Type Description Session

A Session you can use to search, build queries, and export data.

Raises:

Type Description PicSureError

If the token is invalid, the server is unreachable, or the platform name is not recognized.

Example

import picsure session = picsure.connect( ... platform="BDC Authorized", ... token="your-api-token", ... ) You're successfully connected to BDC Authorized as user you@email.com! Your token expires on 2026-06-15T00:00:00Z.

docs.title: Open-access: no token needed
docs.text: session = picsure.connect(platform=picsure.Platform.BDC_OPEN)

docs.title: Platform
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Known PIC-SURE deployment platforms.

Each member stores a :class:PlatformConfig. BDC Authorized and BDC Open share a domain and are distinguished by resource UUID. include_consents controls whether dictionary-api requests must carry the user's consent list; requires_auth controls whether the connection needs a PIC-SURE token at all.

Pass a member to :func:picsure.connect to connect to a known platform, or pass a custom URL string for unlisted deployments.

docs.title: BDC_AUTHORIZED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
BDC_AUTHORIZED = PlatformConfig(
    url="https://picsure.biodatacatalyst.nhlbi.nih.gov",
    resource_uuid="02e23f52-f354-4e8b-992c-d37c8b9ba140",
    label="BDC Authorized",
    include_consents=True,
    requires_auth=True,
)

docs.title: BDC_OPEN class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
BDC_OPEN = PlatformConfig(
    url="https://picsure.biodatacatalyst.nhlbi.nih.gov",
    resource_uuid="ac004461-1b47-4832-80e2-22a4aecabe39",
    label="BDC Open",
    include_consents=False,
    requires_auth=False,
)

docs.title: BDC_DEV_AUTHORIZED class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text:
BDC_DEV_AUTHORIZED = PlatformConfig(
    url="https://dev.picsure.biodatacatalyst.nhlbi.nih.gov",
    resource_uuid="02e23f52-f354-4e8b-992c-d37c8b9ba140",
    label="BDC Authorized",
    include_consents=True,
    requires_auth=True,
)

docs.title: BDC_PREDEV_OPEN class-attribute instance-attribute

    url="https://predev.picsure.biodatacatalyst.nhlbi.nih.gov",
    resource_uuid="ac004461-1b47-4832-80e2-22a4aecabe39",

    url="https://nhanes.hms.harvard.edu/",
    resource_uuid="ded89b08-faa9-435c-b7c4-55b81922ee5f",

    requires_auth=True,

    url="https://nhanes.hms.harvard.edu/",
    resource_uuid="ded89b08-faa9-435c-b7c4-55b81922ee5f",
    label="Nhanes Open",
    include_consents=False,
    requires_auth=False,

docs.title: url property
docs.text: url: str

docs.title: resource_uuid property
docs.text: resource_uuid: str

docs.title: label property
docs.text: label: str

docs.title: include_consents property
docs.text: include_consents: bool

docs.title: requires_auth property
docs.text: requires_auth: bool

docs.title: createClause
docs.text:
createClause(
    keys: str | list[str],
    type: ClauseType,
    categories: str | list[str] | None = None,
    min: float | None = None,
    max: float | None = None,
) -> Clause

Create a single filter clause for use in a query.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default keys str | list[str]

Concept path(s) this clause applies to.

required type ClauseType

The kind of filter. Use ClauseType.FILTER for categorical or range filters, ClauseType.ANYRECORD to match the presence of any value.

required categories str | list[str] | None

For FILTER clauses on categorical variables.

None min float | None

For FILTER clauses on numeric variables, minimum value.

None max float | None

For FILTER clauses on numeric variables, maximum value.

None

Returns:

Type Description Clause

A Clause that can be used in buildClauseGroup() or

Clause

passed directly to Session.runQuery().

Raises:

Type Description PicSureValidationError

If the clause configuration is invalid.

Example

from picsure import createClause, ClauseType sex = createClause( ... r"\phs1\pht1\phv1\sex\", ... type=ClauseType.FILTER, ... categories="Male", ... ) age = createClause( ... r"\phs1\pht1\phv5\age\", ... type=ClauseType.FILTER, ... min=40.0, ... )

docs.title: buildClauseGroup
docs.text:
buildClauseGroup(
    clauses: list[Clause | ClauseGroup],
    root: GroupOperator = GroupOperator.AND,
) -> ClauseGroup

Create a group of clauses combined with AND or OR.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default clauses list[Clause | ClauseGroup]

List of Clause or ClauseGroup objects to combine.

required root GroupOperator

Logical operator — GroupOperator.AND (default) or GroupOperator.OR.

AND

Returns:

Type Description ClauseGroup

A ClauseGroup that can be nested or passed to

ClauseGroup

Session.runQuery().

Raises:

Type Description PicSureValidationError

If the clause list is empty.

Example

from picsure import buildClauseGroup, GroupOperator group = buildClauseGroup( ... [sex_filter, age_filter], ... root=GroupOperator.AND, ... )

docs.title: ClauseType
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Type of filter clause in a PIC-SURE query.

Use these constants with picsure.createClause():

  • FILTER — filter by categorical values or numeric range
  • ANYRECORD — match records where the concept path or any descendant has a value (wire: ANY_RECORD_OF)
  • SELECT — include the concept path(s) in query output; does not contribute to filtering
  • REQUIRE — require the concept path to have a non-null value (wire: REQUIRED)

docs.title: FILTER class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: FILTER = 'filter'

docs.title: ANYRECORD class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: ANYRECORD = 'anyrecord'

docs.title: SELECT class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: SELECT = 'select'

docs.title: REQUIRE class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: REQUIRE = 'require'

docs.title: GroupOperator
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Logical operator for combining clauses in a group.

  • AND — all clauses must match
  • OR — at least one clause must match

docs.title: AND class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: AND = 'AND'

docs.title: OR class-attribute instance-attribute
docs.text: OR = 'OR'

docs.title: Clause dataclass
docs.text:
Clause(
    keys: list[str],
    type: ClauseType,
    categories: list[str] | None = None,
    min: float | None = None,
    max: float | None = None,
)

A single filter clause in a PIC-SURE query.

Created by picsure.createClause(). Can be passed directly to Session.runQuery() or combined with other clauses via picsure.buildClauseGroup().

Wire format. :meth:to_query_json emits a v3 PhenotypicFilter leaf (or an OR PhenotypicSubquery of leaves for multi-key clauses) per the /picsure/v3/query/sync contract.

docs.title: to_query_json
docs.text:
to_query_json() -> dict[str, object]

Serialize this clause as a v3 PhenotypicClause.

Emits a PhenotypicFilter leaf for single-key clauses, or an OR PhenotypicSubquery of per-key leaves when the clause spans multiple keys.

Raises:

Type Description PicSureValidationError

If this is a SELECT clause. SELECT clauses don't participate in filtering; extract their paths via :meth:select_paths instead.

docs.title: ClauseGroup dataclass
docs.text:
ClauseGroup(
    clauses: list[Clause | ClauseGroup],
    operator: GroupOperator,
)

A group of clauses combined with AND or OR.

Created by picsure.buildClauseGroup(). Can contain both Clause and nested ClauseGroup objects for arbitrarily deep nesting.

Wire format. :meth:to_query_json emits a v3 PhenotypicSubquery (operator / phenotypicClauses) per the /picsure/v3/query/sync contract. The previous wire format is not supported.

docs.title: to_query_json
docs.text:
to_query_json() -> dict[str, object]

Serialize this group as a v3 PhenotypicSubquery.

Raises:

Type Description PicSureValidationError

If any nested child is a SELECT clause. SELECT clauses do not participate in filtering and must be kept outside of ClauseGroup so they can be lifted into the top-level select array. Extract SELECT paths via :meth:select_paths before calling :meth:to_query_json.

docs.title: Query module-attribute
docs.text:
Query: TypeAlias = Clause | ClauseGroup

A query is either a single Clause or a ClauseGroup of nested clauses.

docs.title: FacetSet
docs.text:
FacetSet(available: list[FacetCategory])

Mutable container for facet selections.

Created by Session.facets(). Add selections with add(), then pass the FacetSet to Session.search() to narrow results.

docs.title: add
docs.text:
add(category: str, values: str | list[str]) -> None

Add values to a facet category.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default category str

The facet category name (e.g. "study_ids").

required values str | list[str]

One or more values to select.

required

Raises:

Type Description PicSureValidationError

If the category is not valid.

docs.title: view
docs.text:
view() -> dict[str, list[str]]

Return current selections as a dict of category -> selected values.

docs.title: clear
docs.text:
clear(category: str | None = None) -> None

Clear selections. If category is given, clear only that category.

docs.title: CountResult dataclass
docs.text:
CountResult(
    value: int | None,
    margin: int | None,
    cap: int | None,
    raw: str,
)

A count returned by PIC-SURE, preserving obfuscation metadata.

Open-access backends may return a count with one of three shapes:

  • exact: value set; margin and cap are None
  • noisy: value and margin set (server emitted "N ±M")
  • suppressed: value is None; cap set (server emitted "< N")

The raw field preserves the original server string for debugging and for callers that want to re-format the value themselves.

docs.title: value instance-attribute
docs.text: value: int | None

docs.title: margin instance-attribute
docs.text: margin: int | None

docs.title: cap instance-attribute
docs.text: cap: int | None

docs.title: raw instance-attribute
docs.text: raw: str

docs.title: obfuscated property
docs.text:
obfuscated: bool

True if the server returned a noisy or suppressed value.

docs.title: Session
docs.text:
Session(
    client: PicSureClient,
    user_email: str,
    token_expiration: str,
    resources: list[Resource],
    resource_uuid: str | None = None,
    consents: list[str] | None = None,
    total_concepts: int = 0,
    dev_config: DevConfig | None = None,
)

A live connection to a PIC-SURE instance.

Returned by picsure.connect(). Holds the authenticated HTTP client and resource metadata. Methods for search, query building, and export are added in later plans.

docs.title: consents property
docs.text:
consents: list[str]

Study-consent identifiers the user is authorized for.

Empty list on open-access deployments. Dictionary-api calls on authorized deployments must include this list in the request body so the backend scopes results to accessible studies.

docs.title: total_concepts property
docs.text:
total_concepts: int

Total number of concepts in the data dictionary.

Captured at connect time and used as the page size for search requests so every result comes back in one page.

docs.title: search
docs.text:
search(
    term: str = "",
    *,
    facets: FacetSet | None = None,
    include_values: bool = True,
) -> pd.DataFrame

Search the PIC-SURE data dictionary.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default term str

Search term. Empty string returns all variables.

'' facets FacetSet | None

Optional FacetSet to narrow results by category.

None include_values bool

If False, omit variable values from results.

True

Returns:

Type Description DataFrame

DataFrame of matching data dictionary entries.

Example

df = session.search("blood pressure") df_filtered = session.search("sex", facets=my_facets)

docs.title: facets
docs.text:
facets(
    term: str = "", *, facets: FacetSet | None = None
) -> FacetSet

Fetch available facet categories and return a FacetSet.

The returned FacetSet starts with no selections. Use add() to select values, then pass it to search() to narrow results.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default term str

Optional search term. When supplied, each returned category's option counts reflect only concepts matching the term. When omitted (the default), counts are global.

'' facets FacetSet | None

Optional current-selection :class:FacetSet. When supplied, the returned counts reflect how many additional concepts each option would match given the current selections — matching the UI sidebar's behavior.

None

Returns:

Type Description FacetSet

A fresh :class:FacetSet whose available categories carry

FacetSet

contextual counts when term/facets are provided and

FacetSet

global counts otherwise.

Example

fs = session.facets() fs.add("dataset_id", "phs000007") df = session.search("sex", facets=fs)

docs.title: Recompute with contextual counts:
docs.text: refreshed = session.facets(term="sex", facets=fs)

docs.title: showAllFacets
docs.text:
showAllFacets(
    term: str = "", *, facets: FacetSet | None = None
) -> pd.DataFrame

Fetch and display all available facet categories as a DataFrame.

Returns every facet option including those with count 0. The UI hides count=0 options; this method exposes them so notebook callers can see which options matched nothing.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default term str

Optional search term; when provided, the returned counts are contextual to concepts matching the term.

'' facets FacetSet | None

Optional current-selection :class:FacetSet; when provided, counts reflect what would remain if each option were added to the current selection.

None

Returns:

Type Description DataFrame

DataFrame with columns: category, Category Display,

DataFrame

display, description, value, count. Counts

DataFrame

are contextual when term/facets are supplied; global

DataFrame

otherwise.

docs.title: runQuery
docs.text:
runQuery(
    query: Query, type: QueryType | str = "count"
) -> CountResult | dict[str, CountResult] | pd.DataFrame

Execute a query and return the result.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default query Query

A Clause or ClauseGroup built with createClause/buildClauseGroup.

required type QueryType | str

Result type. Pass either a :class:QueryType member (e.g. QueryType.COUNT) or one of the strings:

  • "count" → :class:CountResult with value / margin / cap fields, preserving server-side obfuscation of small counts.
  • "cross_count" → dict[str, CountResult] keyed by concept path.
  • "participant" → :class:pandas.DataFrame.
  • "timestamp" → :class:pandas.DataFrame.
'count'

Returns:

Name Type Description A CountResult | dict[str, CountResult] | DataFrame

class:CountResult, a dict[str, CountResult], or a

CountResult | dict[str, CountResult] | DataFrame

DataFrame depending on type.

Example

count = session.runQuery(my_query, type="count") if count.value is not None: ... print(f"{count.value} participants") ... else: ... print(f"fewer than {count.cap} participants") df = session.runQuery(my_query, type="participant")
